title: Fleischmannia trinervia

The genus Fleischmannia is a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America, and is closely related to other genera within the tribe Eupatorieae. The exact classification within the tribe is not well-documented, but it is generally accepted that Fleischmannia trinervia belongs to the family Asteraceae. intro: Fleischmannia trinervia, also known as "Trinidad fleamania" or "Hierba mora", is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fleischmannia trinervia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in folk medicine to make topical applications for skin and wound care.
